WarTranslated Satellite imagery of the Russian naval base in Novorossiysk following the strike on the night of April 6. The images show damage to the bow of the frigate Admiral Essen, a carrier of "Kalibr" cruise missiles. One of the piers was also damaged. Following the strike, an oil spill was detected in the naval harbor, leaking into the Black Sea. An oil loading berth near the port was also damaged. tweet
OSINTdefender RT @DanLamothe : The number of U.S. troops wounded in the war with Iran now exceeds 370, according to a Pentagon tally. About two-thirds of those service members are in the Army. tweet

Faytuks News RT @manniefabian : The IDF calls on vessels off the coast of southern Lebanon to sail north toward Tyre. "Hezbollah activity endangers vessels in the maritime area between Tyre and Ras Naqoura, which compels the IDF to act against it in the maritime domain," says army spokesman Col. Avichay Adraee. He says that "all vessels anchored or sailing in the maritime area marked on the map must immediately sail north of the Tyre area." tweet

The US war against Iran is costing about $500 million per day, totaling roughly $22–31 billion over five weeks so far. Around $2–3.6 billion of that is from destroyed or damaged military equipment. Source: FT

🇮🇷 💬 Iran has laid down a clear rule of engagement: strike Iranian energy facilities, and equivalent facilities across the Gulf get targeted. An Arab analyst breaks down exactly what's at stake: 🔸 Gulf states hold 1.55 trillion barrels — 32% of global oil reserves 🔸 Targeting these facilities could eliminate 20% of global oil supply — 18 million barrels per day 🔸 20% of global LNG demand comes from this region 🔸 25-30% of global urea fertilizer exports — food supply implications for years 🇸🇦 KSA: Ghawar field (58B barrels), Safaniya field (34B barrels), Ras Tanura port (handles 90% of Saudi exports), Jubail petrochemical complex, Sadara chemicals 🇦🇪 UAE: Zakum field (1M bbl/day), Ruwais refinery (817K bbl/day), Fujairah port, Barakah nuclear plant (5,600 MW) 🇶🇦 Qatar: North Field (2,000 trillion cubic feet — world's largest gas field), Ras Laffan port and refinery 🇰🇼 Kuwait: Az-Zour refinery (615K bbl/day), Greater Burgan field, Ahmadi port All of these facilities involve American companies. That is the point. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

🇨🇳 Chinese Foreign Ministry Spokesman Lin Jian: "The situation in the Middle East continues to worsen and the military conflict is still escalating, hitting the world economy and energy security and drawing deep concerns from the international community. China has been working to help bring about a ceasefire and end to the conflict. The root cause of the conflict is the U.S.-Israeli launch of military attacks against Iran in violation of international law. The top priority is to bring an immediate end to the military operations and return to dialogue and negotiation, so as to fundamentally resolve the issues and restore peace and stability in the Gulf region." 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

Trump is described by senior officials as emboldened and “in a mode of defiance,” proud of a recent rescue operation and open—but not expecting—a diplomatic deal with Iran. The administration plans to keep “maximum pressure” on Iran, with no final decision yet on next steps if a deal isn’t reached. Source: WSJ

JD Vance: In Western history, they don’t see a proud tradition; they see only injustice. Within our borders, they see exclusion and racism. In Christianity, they see not liberation but oppression. And in the family, they see constraint. Their most radical members, of course, tear down monuments to our national heroes or throw buckets of fake blood on precious artworks in museums.
